pattern: Sorting reveals the trick: sacrifice the smallest third as triple-bottoms, then harvest medians from every second element of the surviving top two-thirds.
hints: Each triple wastes its smallest element below the median, so the global smallest values are the natural sacrifices.
After setting aside the smallest third as bottoms, pair the rest so each median is as large as possible.
Sort ascending; the answer sums the elements at positions n-2, n-4, ... for n/3 terms.

stress.description: random array of length divisible by three
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = max(1, min(size, 300)) // 3 or 1
    n = 3 * m
    nums = [rng.randrange(1, 10**6 + 1) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
stress.note: length forced to a multiple of three
